mission.title: Install and Configure Kcp on Kubernetes
mission.description: kcp is a Kubernetes-like control plane designed for managing multiple isolated clusters, known as workspaces. It enables API service providers to centrally offer APIs and facilitates easy API consumption for users within their workspaces. Installing kcp allows you to leverage its capabilities for multi-tenant applications and services.

steps.title: Add the kcp Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official kcp Helm repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add kcp https://github.com/kcp-dev/kcp/releases/download/v0.30.0
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install kcp using Helm
steps.description:
Install kcp in a new namespace called 'kcp-system':
```bash
helm install kcp kcp/kcp --namespace kcp-system --create-namespace --version v0.30.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the kcp pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kcp-system
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for kcp
steps.description:
Edit the kcp deployment to set resource limits:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kcp --namespace kcp-system
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   requests:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
#   limits:
#     cpu: "1"
#     memory: "1Gi"
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for kcp
steps.description:
Create a ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding for kcp:
```bash
kubectl create clusterrole kcp-admin --verb=* --resource=* 

kubectl create clusterrolebinding kcp-admin-binding --clusterrole=kcp-admin --serviceaccount=kcp-system:kcp
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of kcp will show the kcp pods running in the 'kcp-system' namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and RBAC for the kcp deployment, ensuring it operates efficiently and securely.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kcp https://github.com/kcp-dev/kcp/releases/download/v0.30.0
helm repo update
helm install kcp kcp/kcp --namespace kcp-system --create-namespace --version v0.30.0

kubectl edit deployment kcp --namespace kcp-system
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   requests:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
#   limits:
#     cpu: "1"
#     memory: "1Gi"

kubectl create clusterrole kcp-admin --verb=* --resource=* 

kubectl create clusterrolebinding kcp-admin-binding --clusterrole=kcp-admin --serviceaccount=kcp-system:kcp

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
